preFix.title: Repairing a broken drawer runner
preFix.subtitle: Replace or reinforce a worn or broken side-mount or bottom-run drawer slide on wooden furniture
preFix.excerpt: How to repair a drawer that sticks, drops at the front, or falls out. Covers diagnosing whether the runner, kicker, or guide is the problem, and the fixes for traditional wooden side-runners and modern metal side-mount slides.

content.text: A sticking or dropping drawer is almost always a worn or broken

, the timber has worn hollow where the drawer sides or drawer bottom have rubbed over decades of use. The repair is straightforward: strip the worn runner, glue on a new section, plane level, wax, and refit.

Remove the drawer and examine the runner inside the carcase. Run a finger along the surface, a worn hollow is easy to feel. If the runner is smooth but the drawer sticks, the problem is swelling or paint build-up on the drawer sides: plane or sand the drawer sides rather than the runner. If the drawer drops at the front when pulled out, check whether there is a kicker above the drawer, it may have broken off.
Step 2, Remove and replace the worn runner section

Chisel or plane off the top surface of the worn runner to give a flat gluing surface. Cut a new strip of timber (matching hardwood or a close-grain pine) to the same width and length as the runner. Glue it to the cleared surface with PVA and hold in position with cramps or screws until cured (4 hours minimum). Once cured, plane the new runner flush with the surrounding carcase surface, the drawer must slide without any bump at the join.

Rub a candle or a block of beeswax along the new runner and along the drawer sides and bottom edges. Wax is the traditional lubricant for wooden drawers and lasts years. Refit the drawer and test: it should slide smoothly with no catching and no front-drop. If it is too tight, plane a shaving off the drawer side that is catching, mark the tight spots by rubbing chalk on the runner and closing the drawer; the chalk transfers to the tight spots.

Modern metal side-mount slides: most failures are a bent slide arm or a broken plastic bracket at the back of the carcase. Replacement slides are sold by length in 10 mm increments; the key measurement is the internal carcase depth. Remove both slides (they unclip or unscrew), fit the new pair, and check the drawer is level before loading.

initialFindings.message: PATTERN tutorial has no Method/Steps section and no orderedList — sequential instructions missing
tricolon: tricolon (three parallel items) — two adjectives almost always beats three
